question.text: Under SEBI SAST Regulations 2011, an acquirer holding 30% voting rights along with PAC sells 3% during the financial year and then buys back 4%. What is the gross acquisition counted for the creeping acquisition limit?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SAST 2011, gross acquisitions are counted for the creeping acquisition limit, regardless of any intermittent sales or dilution. The acquirer purchased 4% during the year, so the gross acquisition is 4% — the 3% sale is irrelevant for this calculation. **The Trap:** Many candidates calculate the net acquisition (4% - 3% = 1%), but SAST explicitly counts gross acquisitions. This prevents gaming through buy-sell-buy strategies to circumvent the 5% annual limit. **Cross-Exam Context:** This "gross vs. net" concept also appears in NISM Series VIII for derivative position limits and in NISM Series XII for securities lending. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always track cumulative gross purchases through the financial year. A client who sells and repurchases is still consuming their 5% creeping acquisition allowance on every purchase.
